overview: This is a Mountain bluebird (Sialia currucoides), a small thrush native to western North America. The bird shown is likely an adult male, characterized by its bright turquoise-blue plumage and thin black bill. They are known for foraging by hovering over the ground and pouncing on insect prey from elevated perches like the tree branch shown. These birds are the official state bird of both Idaho and Nevada. iNaturalist +6 6 sites Mountain bluebird - Wikipedia

This is a Mountain bluebird (Sialia currucoides), a small thrush native to western North America.
The bird shown is likely an adult male, characterized by its bright turquoise-blue plumage and thin black bill.
They are known for foraging by hovering over the ground and pouncing on insect prey from elevated perches like the tree branch shown.
These birds are the official state bird of both Idaho and Nevada.
